agent: double-well explorer dx=(x-x^3)dt+sigma dW, tracked to x_ref=0
preregistered_criterion: both curves monotone and graded (D_ag->0, lambda->-inf)
verdict: CLAIM CONFIRMED: both curves are monotone and graded. D_ag falls smoothly 0.85 -> 0.0119 (approaching the annihilation limit D_ag -> 0) and lambda_parallel drops -0.09 -> -11.5 toward -infinity as gain rises. The double-well bifurcation at k=1 is washed out by the exploratory noise, so the agency cost is genuinely graded -- no discontinuity, no non-monotonicity. Sec 7.3's 'graded, monotone' finite-gain cost is numerically vindicated.
